objective: Assess whether storage-cavity active cooling based on the transmon |f> manifold and the |g,n> <-> |f,n-1> ladder is spectrally accessible, controllable, and dissipative in the local cqed_sim device for n <= 4.
completed_tasks: Initialized the study folder, README, improvement log, and run-control scaffold.
Audited the local cqed_sim package for sideband-drive support, Hamiltonian conventions, and open-system readout decay.
Implemented the shared study helpers, runtime compatibility shim, end-to-end analysis script, convergence-check script, and notebook builder.
Extracted the dressed storage-conversion and readout-dump frequencies through n=4 and exported machine-readable tables.
Completed pulse scans across multiple envelope families and selected practical-best storage and dump pulses.
Simulated single-cycle and repeated-cycle open-system cooling on basis, coherent, and thermal-like initial states.
Generated report-quality figures, technical report, compiled PDF, and executed reproducibility notebook.

key_results.main_conclusion: The effective storage g-f sideband plus readout-assisted dump is usable through n<=4 in the local model, while a direct transmon g-f carrier is not a cooling mechanism.
